summary:
Volume has pulled back 13% over the last four weeks — eight runs versus ten in the prior block — which fits a natural consolidation pattern, particularly after the 44 km effort on June 8th that carried a load of 355, the heaviest single session in the recent window by a significant margin. Yesterday's 11.7 km at 5:59/km and HR 143 looks like appropriate recovery pacing off the back of that workload distribution.

The June 27th run stands out as the quality anchor of the recent block: 15.7 km at 4:49/km with HR 161, sitting meaningfully above the other aerobic sessions in both pace and cardiac cost. With your current LT pegged at 4:24/km @ 178 bpm as of today, that effort was operating in the upper aerobic/threshold-adjacent zone — useful stimulus, and worth tracking whether you can sustain that pace band as volume rebuilds.

Daily steps averaging 13,940 — with rest days still holding above 11,400 — reflects solid non-running movement load, which matters for ultra preparation. Strength frequency (four sessions in four weeks) is functional but on the leaner side given the 34 YTD sessions; if the current three-week streak is a deliberate rebuild of consistency, maintaining that cadence into the next block would be the priority.
